ead.archdesc:

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Ithaca Womans Club records
Ithaca Woman's Club (Ithaca, N.Y.)
2743
4.4 cubic feet. 4.4 cubic feet.
1897-1963. Manuscript volumes, receipts, programs, historical summaries, news clippings, and a scrapbook. Records include three manuscript volumes containing constitution, by-laws, list of charter members; treasurers' records (1897-1909); and minutes of the Home Economic Section of the Ithaca Woman's Club (1902). Also miscellaneous items including receipts, programs, historical summaries, news clippings. One scrapbook containing a history of the club, lists of charter and early members, photographs of club presidents and copies of presidents' speeches and club brochures (1897-1949). Collection material in English 2315924
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
B. Shepherd
Date completed:
July 1967
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, May 2003
Date modified:
RMC Staff, January 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Records include three manuscript volumes containing constitution, by-laws, list of charter members; treasurers' records (1897-1909); and minutes of the Home Economic Section of the Ithaca Woman's Club (1902). Also miscellaneous items including receipts, programs, historical summaries, news clippings. One scrapbook containing a history of the club, lists of charter and early members, photographs of club presidents and copies of presidents' speeches and club brochures (1897-1949).
Cite As:
Ithaca Woman's Club records, #2743. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scrapbooks. Minutes. Clippings (information artifacts) Tompkins County (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Social life and customs. Women -- Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Societies and clubs. Home economics. Ithaca Woman's Club (Ithaca, N.Y.). Home Economic Section
2315924 <<<<<<< HEAD Large scrapbook compiled by Mrs. A.E.M. Hasbrouck and presented to the Ithaca Woman's Club in 1928. Contains typed history of the Club, list of Charter and Earliest Members for as far as was known at that date, photographs of:19281
Scope and Contents
Mrs. Hasbrouck
Mrs. Charles Crandall
Miss Charity Smith
Mrs. Louisa Riley (2) and her home on University Avenue
The Lewis Home, 444 N. Aurora St. (where Club was organized)
Later photographs include:
Mrs. Jamie Messenger
Juanita B. Bates
Mrs. J.A. Winans
Lelle Rhodes Mangang
Mary M. Denton
Mrs. Sidney L. Howell
Sarah L. Osborn
Viola B. Lent
Maude Coleman Walker
Margaret L. Head
Mrs. Thomas J. Parker
Alice D. Smiley
Anna Ball Cary (Mrs. Elmer E.)
Gratia Mungle
Isabel B. Doyle
Mrs. Alfred H. Butwell
Mrs. David Perry
Minnie W. Muenscher
Veta A. Butterworth
Mrs. A.L. Winsor
and groups.
(All the foregoing were presidents.)
This scrapbook also contains copies of the presidents' speeches and brochures of the club through various years. The first program is dated 1896-1897 and series runs through to 1948-1949."New Crusader" Yearbooks + Journal Writeups1915-192521Meeting Minutes Ithaca Women's Club03/1909 - 03/191422Secretary's Report of Meetings of Ithaca Women's Club04/1914-03/192123Meeting Minutes Ithaca Women's Club. Note: Minutes from Oct 3 1921 to Sept 1942 were burnt when Secretary left town (with approval of President)10/1921-05/193224Meeting Minutes Ithaca Women's Club09/1921-12/194725Meeting Minutes Ithaca active/current members34Card catalogue of former, life and deceased members35Social Chairman's Records1935-198036Minutes for 5th Household Arts Group of Women's Club1949-197337Treasurer?s Reports Household Arts1967-197938Second Division Household Arts Minutes + Old Correspondence1947-196839Corresponding Secretary cash records notebook1911-1912310First Literature Department Notebook1956-1961311Treasure Report1966-1971312Antique Study Group Programs1957-1981313Second Drama Division Annual Report1978-1994314Second Literature Department Annual Program1978-1992315Second Literature Department Notebook1963-1966316Second Literature Department Programs + Notebook1983-1991317Second Literature Department Notebook1938-194641Antique Study Group Minutes and Attendance Records1949-197042Household Arts Group1983-199743Chairman of Committees Bylaws197744Committee Reports1969-197945Community Improvement Project1964-196646First Literature Department Meeting Book1950-195647Secretary-Treasurer's Book of the Second Division of Household Arts1946-195148First Literature Group Treasury Book1976-198649Literature Department Book1963-1976410Women's Community Building Treasurer's Reports1997-1999411Community Improvement Program Report1998-2000412Ithaca Women?s Club Receipts Book1962-1972413Ithaca Women's Club Receipts Book1972-1989414Income and Expenses Ledger1989-1990415Awards and Memoranda I1968-2005416Awards and Memoranda II1968-2005417Second Drama Division Notebook1980-1990418In Remembrance Book1957-2009419Secretary Book1961-1981420Parliamentary Practice Book1921421Burgess Excerpts422Scrapbook1925-19465Scrapbook1946-19625Scrapbook1966-19695Small manuscript volume of Constitution and By-Laws, also contains list of charter members. (This volume has the following inscription: "This is to record that this constitution is in my father's handwriting" (signed) Howard Wait Riley, June 5, 1967). This volume also contains the Revised Constitution of June 1, 19035Small manuscript volume - Treasurer's Book1897-19095IWC Constitution1915-19165Minute book of the Home Economic Section of the Ithaca Woman's Club19025
Scope and Contents
This book consists of typed and mimeographed copies of minutes of meetings, outlines of talks given by various people on different topics such as:
Development of the Home
Books and Pictures in the Home
Artistic Homes
Some Advantages of Vegetarianism (etc.)
People mentioned as giving talks:
Martha Van Rensselaer
Mrs. Effie D. Morse
Mrs. John Gifford
Professor Carpenter
Dr. Wilder
Prof. V.A. Moore
Mrs. M.V. SlingerlandTwo page handwritten information about Mrs. Louisa L. Riley, organizer of the Ithaca Woman's Club in 189551Receipted bill for $15.00 for programs from Atkinson and Mitchell190751Receipt from New York State Federation of Women's Clubs for dues1908-190951Receipt from Western New York Federation of Women's Clubs190951Ithaca Woman's Club, Twentieth Anniversary Dinner program191451Constitution and By-Laws, Ithaca Woman's Club, revised192451Note about the gavel presented to the Club by Mrs. E.A. Denton193151Poem by Mary M. Denton, written originally for the Woman's Club Conservation Dinner, 1918-04-29, seven months before the Armistice was signed. Repeated at the fortieth anniversary1918-04-291935-12-251Ten pages (typed), "A History of the Woman's Club," written for the Fortieth Anniversary Banquet, Mary M. Denton1935-12-0251Four pages (carbon typescript), about the Woman's Club, written for the Fortieth Anniversary, by (Mrs.) G.D. Hopkins.1935-12-0251News clipping from New York Times, regarding the founding of the Woman's Club of Orange, New Jersey, at Mrs. Louisa L. Riley's home in 1872193951Four page carbon typescript, "History of the Ithaca Woman's Club," written by Ada E. Hasbrouck, a charter member. For the Fortieth Anniversary in . Contains list of Charter and Earliest Members, so far as known194051Letter to "Dear Maude" from Alice M (?- née Myers?)1945-05-0951Resolutions (carbon typescript) adopted at Club meeting1963-04-0151Two negatives of the group pictures in Scrapbook51 ======= Large scrapbook compiled by Mrs. A.E.M. Hasbrouck and presented to the Ithaca Woman's Club in 1928. Contains typed history of the Club, list of Charter and Earliest Members for as far as was known at that date, photographs of:19281
Scope and Contents
Mrs. Hasbrouck
Mrs. Charles Crandall
Miss Charity Smith
Mrs. Louisa Riley (2) and her home on University Avenue
The Lewis Home, 444 N. Aurora St. (where Club was organized)
Later photographs include:
Mrs. Jamie Messenger
Juanita B. Bates
Mrs. J.A. Winans
Lelle Rhodes Mangang
Mary M. Denton
Mrs. Sidney L. Howell
Sarah L. Osborn
Viola B. Lent
Maude Coleman Walker
Margaret L. Head
Mrs. Thomas J. Parker
Alice D. Smiley
Anna Ball Cary (Mrs. Elmer E.)
Gratia Mungle
Isabel B. Doyle
Mrs. Alfred H. Butwell
Mrs. David Perry
Minnie W. Muenscher
Veta A. Butterworth
Mrs. A.L. Winsor
and groups.
(All the foregoing were presidents.)
This scrapbook also contains copies of the presidents' speeches and brochures of the club through various years.
